notes.anchor: If you and the army are well, it is well
notes.note: S. v. b. e. v.: si vos valetis, bene est, valeo --- the formal opening of an official letter to a governor with troops in his province. Cicero does not use it in private correspondence.

Paludamentum: the red military cloak worn by a magistrate setting out for his province under imperium. The departure was a recognised public ceremony at which a man's friends accompanied him to the city gate.

Societas publicanorum: a tax-farming company. Cuspius had served as a senior officer of the dominant African societas, presumably collecting the stipendium and other revenues, and so had built up a network of provincial clients.
